summary: I compiled the three review critics' verdicts into a single PR-ready review report at `.pipeliner/phases/04-review/main/review-report-writer/output/review_report.md`. Before writing, I verified every claim against the current code: the `requirements-conformance-critic` passes all 50 requirements (R1–R50); the `code-quality-critic`'s recorded `needs_work` verdict rested on four performance/consistency findings, all of which I confirmed are now fixed in the working tree (the `dashboard:` flag in `broadcast_card.rb`, DB-level `ORDER … LIMIT` in `recent_activity.rb`, the union stuck+unstuck broadcast in `sweep.rb`, and the single guarded broadcast in `manager_tick.rb`); and the `guide-alignment-critic`'s `needs_work` verdict rests on the five minor UI/style-guide items (F1–F5), which I confirmed are still present in the current build. The report is structured as what was asked → what was built → evidence of conformance (per critic, with test/lint status) → a resolved-findings table for the fixed code-quality items → an open-findings table for the five minor conformance items → a merge recommendation that flags UI-5 (fleet polling vs. Turbo Streams) as needing an explicit manager decision and the system-test CI run as an environmental follow-up.
